common.oneLiner: Convert salary ↔ hourly, see your federal + provincial tax breakdown, and compare take-home pay across all 13 Canadian provinces — instantly.
common.disclaimer: This calculator provides estimates based on {year} CRA tax tables. Actual deductions may vary.

meta.homeDescription: Free Canadian salary calculator. Convert salary ↔ hourly, see federal + provincial tax breakdown, compare take-home pay across all 13 provinces. Updated for {year}.
meta.compareTitle: Compare Take-Home Pay Across Canadian Provinces | PayCalc
meta.compareDescription: Compare your salary after taxes across Canadian provinces. See which province gives you the highest take-home pay.
meta.taxBracketsTitle: Canadian Tax Brackets {year} — Federal & Provincial | PayCalc
meta.taxBracketsDescription: Complete {year} Canadian tax brackets for federal and all 13 provincial/territorial income tax rates.
meta.minimumWageTitle: Minimum Wage by Province {year} — Canada | PayCalc
meta.minimumWageDescription: Current minimum wage rates across all Canadian provinces and territories for {year}.
meta.provinceDescription: Calculate your take-home pay in {province}. See {year} provincial tax brackets, deductions, and net income.
meta.incomeStructureTitle: Income Structure Comparison {year} — Salary vs Corp vs Sole Prop | PayCalc
meta.incomeStructureDescription: Compare take-home pay as a salaried employee, sole proprietor, or incorporated (CCPC) in Canada. Find the best income structure for {year}.
meta.incomeTitle: Canadian Investment Income Tax Calculator {year} | PayCalc.ca
meta.incomeDescription: Calculate tax on employment income, capital gains, eligible and ineligible dividends, and interest income across all Canadian provinces. Free, bilingual, instant results.

incomeStructure.title: Income Structure Comparison
incomeStructure.subtitle: Compare take-home pay across employment types: salaried, sole proprietorship, and incorporated
incomeStructure.expenseTooltip: Percentage of gross revenue spent on business expenses (rent, supplies, etc.)

income.pageDescription: Calculate your tax on employment, dividends, capital gains, and other income
income.capitalGainsNote: Enter the total capital gain. 50% will be included in taxable income.
income.eligibleDividendsNote: Dividends from public Canadian corporations (T5 Box 24).
income.ineligibleDividendsNote: Dividends from private corporations / CCPCs (T5 Box 10).
income.employmentIncomeNote: Salary, wages, bonuses, and other employment income.
income.selfEmploymentIncomeNote: Net self-employment income after business expenses.
income.otherIncomeNote: Bank interest, GIC interest, rental income, pensions, EI benefits, etc.
income.noResults: Enter an income amount to see your tax breakdown
income.ctaDescription: If you earn income from investments, dividends, capital gains, or self-employment, our advanced calculator gives you the full picture.
income.ctaPension: Have pension income? Our calculator includes the pension income credit and pension splitting analysis.
income.backToSalary: Only have employment income? Use our salary calculator.
income.disclaimer: This calculator does not account for Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT), which may apply to certain income scenarios.

pension.pensionIncomeNote: Income from a registered pension plan, RRIF, or life annuity. Do not include CPP, OAS, or GIS here.
pension.age65Toggle: Are you 65 or older at December 31?
pension.spouseIncomeNote: Enter your spouse's total income from all other sources excluding the pension being split.
pension.spouseAge65: Spouse is 65 or older at December 31?
pension.spousePensionCredit: Spouse's pension income credit (unlocked through splitting)
pension.splittingDisclaimer: Pension income splitting is an annual election on Form T1032. No money needs to change hands. This calculator does not account for the impact on OAS clawback, GIS eligibility, or other income-tested benefits.
pension.retirementDisclaimer: This calculator does not account for CPP/QPP sharing, OAS clawback, or the spousal amount credit. Consult a tax professional for comprehensive retirement tax planning.

footer.whyAdsText: PayCalc is free to use. Ads help us keep it that way.
footer.notTaxAdvice: For informational purposes only — not tax advice.
